slang_text: It's Paul and Timothy here, servants of Jesus Christ, writin' to all the saints in Christ Jesus out in Philippi, plus the overseers and servants: Grace to you, and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. I thank my God every single time I think of you guys, always throwin' up requests on your behalf, and doin' it with straight-up joy,

web_text: for your partnership in furtherance of the Good News from the first day until now; being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ. It is even right for me to think this way on behalf of all of you, because I have you in my heart, because, both in my bonds and in the defense and confirmation of the Good News, you all are partakers with me of grace. For God is my witness, how I long after all of you in the tender mercies of Christ Jesus.
slang_text: for you guys teamin' up to push the Good News forward, all the way from day one till now; totally confident that whoever kicked off this good work in you is gonna finish it right up till the day of Jesus Christ. It's only right for me to think that way about all of you, 'cause I've got you locked in my heart — whether I'm in chains or out there defendin' and backin' up the Good News, you're all in this grace right alongside me. God's my witness on how much I miss every one of you, with the tender mercy of Christ Jesus.

web_text: This I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and all discernment; so that you may approve the things that are excellent; that you may be sincere and without offense to the day of Christ; being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God. Now I desire to have you know, brothers, that the things which happened to me have turned out rather to the progress of the Good News;
slang_text: Here's what I'm prayin' for: that your love keeps stackin' up more and more, loaded with knowledge and sharp discernment; so you can spot what's truly excellent; so you'll stay sincere and free of blame right up to Christ's day; loaded up with the fruits of righteousness that come through Jesus Christ, to God's glory and praise. Now I want you to know, brothers, that everything that's happened to me has actually turned around and pushed the Good News forward even more;

web_text: so that it became evident to the whole palace guard, and to all the rest, that my bonds are in Christ; and that most of the brothers in the Lord, being confident through my bonds, are more abundantly bold to speak the word of God without fear. Some indeed preach Christ even out of envy and strife, and some also out of good will. The former insincerely preach Christ from selfish ambition, thinking that they add affliction to my chains;
slang_text: so it's become plain to the whole palace guard and everybody else that these chains I'm in are for Christ; and most of the brothers in the Lord, gettin' bolder just from watchin' me stuck in chains, are speakin' God's word way more fearlessly now. Sure, some guys are preachin' Christ outta envy and rivalry, and some outta genuine good will. Them first ones aren't even sincere about it, preachin' Christ for their own selfish ambition, figurin' they're pilin' more grief onto my chains;

web_text: but the latter out of love, knowing that I am appointed for the defense of the Good News. What does it matter? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed. I rejoice in this, yes, and will rejoice. For I know that this will turn out to my salvation, through your prayers and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ, according to my earnest expectation and hope, that I will in no way be disappointed, but with all boldness, as always, now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life, or by death.
slang_text: but the other guys are doin' it outta love, knowin' I'm set here to defend the Good News. But so what? However it goes down, whether it's fake or genuine, Christ gets proclaimed either way. I'm stoked about that, yeah, and I'm gonna keep bein' stoked. 'Cause I know this whole thing's gonna turn out to my salvation, through your prayers and the Spirit of Jesus Christ backin' me up, matchin' up with my hope and expectation that I won't come out lookin' bogus, but with total boldness, same as always, Christ is gonna get magnified through my body, whether that's through livin' or through dyin'.

web_text: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain. But if I live on in the flesh, this will bring fruit from my work; yet I don’t know what I will choose. But I am in a dilemma between the two, having the desire to depart and be with Christ, which is far better. Yet, to remain in the flesh is more needful for your sake.
slang_text: 'Cause for me, livin' is Christ, and dyin' is straight-up profit. But if I keep livin' in this body, that means more fruit from my work — so honestly, I don't even know which one I'd pick. I'm stuck between the two, torn — part of me wants to bounce and be with Christ, which is way better, no question. But stickin' around in this body's more needed, for your sake.

web_text: Having this confidence, I know that I will remain, yes, and remain with you all, for your progress and joy in the faith, that your rejoicing may abound in Christ Jesus in me through my presence with you again. Only let your way of life be worthy of the Good News of Christ, that, whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear of your state, that you stand firm in one spirit, with one soul striving for the faith of the Good News; and in nothing frightened by the adversaries, which is for them a proof of destruction, but to you of salvation, and that from God.
slang_text: With that confidence, I'm bettin' I'm gonna stick around, yeah, stick around with all of you, for your growth and joy in the faith, so your celebratin' can pile up even more in Christ Jesus, once I'm back with you again in person. Just make sure your whole way of livin' matches up with the Good News of Christ, so whether I show up and see you or stay away, I'll hear you're standin' firm in one spirit, one soul, fightin' side by side for the faith of the Good News; and not spooked one bit by your enemies — that's proof they're headed for destruction, but proof you're headed for salvation, straight from God.

web_text: Because it has been granted to you on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in him, but also to suffer on his behalf, having the same conflict which you saw in me, and now hear is in me.
slang_text: 'Cause you've been given a gift for Christ's sake — not just believin' in Him, but sufferin' for Him too, goin' through the exact same fight you already saw me go through, and now you're hearin' I'm still in it.

web_text: If therefore there is any exhortation in Christ, if any consolation of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any tender mercies and compassion, make my joy full, by being like-minded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind; doing nothing through rivalry or through conceit, but in humility, each counting others better than himself; each of you not just looking to his own things, but each of you also to the things of others.
slang_text: So if there's any encouragement goin' on in Christ, any comfort from love, any fellowship in the Spirit, any tender mercy and compassion — make my joy complete: get on the same page, keep the same love, stay in sync, one mind. Don't do nothin' outta rivalry or bein' stuck-up — stay humble, each one of you figurin' the other guy's better than you. Don't just look out for your own stuff — look out for everybody else's stuff too.

web_text: Have this in your mind, which was also in Christ Jesus, who, existing in the form of God, didn’t consider equality with God a thing to be grasped, but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men. And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to death, yes, the death of the cross.
slang_text: Keep this mindset locked in, the same one Christ Jesus had — Him, existin' as God Himself, didn't cling to bein' equal with God like it was somethin' to grab onto. Instead, He emptied Himself out completely, takin' on the form of a servant, born lookin' just like the rest of us. And once He showed up lookin' human, He humbled Himself even further, obeyin' all the way to death — yeah, death on a cross, the ugliest way to go.

web_text: Therefore God also highly exalted him, and gave to him the name which is above every name; that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of those in heaven, those on earth, and those under the earth, and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father. So then, my beloved, even as you have always obeyed, not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
slang_text: That's why God lifted Him up sky-high, and handed Him the name that's above every other name; so at the name of Jesus, every single knee's gonna bow — up in heaven, down here on earth, under the earth too — and every tongue's gonna admit Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father. So then, my beloved, same as you've always obeyed, not just when I'm around but way more now that I'm not — work out your own salvation with fear and tremblin'.

web_text: For it is God who works in you both to will and to work, for his good pleasure. Do all things without murmurings and disputes, that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without defect in the middle of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you are seen as lights in the world, holding up the word of life; that I may have something to boast in the day of Christ, that I didn’t run in vain nor labor in vain.
slang_text: 'Cause it's God workin' in you, both wantin' it and doin' it, all for what He's stoked about. Do everything without grumblin' or arguin', so you turn out blameless and harmless, God's own kids, no flaws, right in the middle of this crooked, bogus generation — where you're supposed to shine like lights out in the world, holdin' up the word of life; so I've got somethin' to brag about come the day of Christ, that I didn't run this race for nothin', didn't work myself to the bone for nothin'.

web_text: Yes, and if I am poured out on the sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice, and rejoice with you all. In the same way, you also rejoice, and rejoice with me. But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, that I also may be cheered up when I know how you are doing. For I have no one else like-minded, who will truly care about you.
slang_text: Yeah, and even if I get poured out like a drink offerin' on top of your faith's sacrifice and service, I'm stoked about it, stoked right along with all of you. Same deal, you should be stoked too, stoked with me. I'm hopin' in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy your way real soon, so I can get cheered up too once I hear how you're doin'. 'Cause I don't got nobody else on the same wavelength who's gonna genuinely care about you like he does.

web_text: For they all seek their own, not the things of Jesus Christ. But you know the proof of him, that, as a child serves a father, so he served with me in furtherance of the Good News. Therefore I hope to send him at once, as soon as I see how it will go with me. But I trust in the Lord that I myself also will come shortly.
slang_text: 'Cause everybody else out there's just chasin' their own agenda, not Jesus Christ's stuff. But you already know Timothy's proven himself — like a kid servin' his own dad, that's how he served alongside me, pushin' the Good News forward. So I'm hopin' to send him out right away, soon as I see how things pan out for me. And I trust in the Lord that I'll be comin' myself before too long too.

web_text: But I counted it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker, fellow soldier, and your apostle and servant of my need; since he longed for you all, and was very troubled, because you had heard that he was sick. For indeed he was sick, nearly to death, but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, that I might not have sorrow on sorrow. I have sent him therefore the more diligently, that, when you see him again, you may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.
slang_text: But I figured it was necessary to send Epaphroditus your way — my brother, fellow worker, fellow soldier, and the guy you sent to look after what I needed. He was achin' to see you all, and gettin' pretty worked up 'cause you'd heard he was sick. And yeah, he really was sick, nearly died on us, but God showed him mercy — and not just him, but me too, so I wouldn't get piled on with grief on top of grief. So I'm sendin' him back extra quick, so when you see him again you can be stoked, and I won't be so bummed out myself.

web_text: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy, and hold such in honor, because for the work of Christ he came near to death, risking his life to supply that which was lacking in your service toward me.
slang_text: So welcome him back in the Lord with all the joy you got, and hold guys like him in high honor, 'cause for Christ's own work he came right up to death's door, riskin' his own life to fill in whatever was missin' in your service to me.

web_text: Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not tiresome, but for you it is safe. Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of the false circumcision. For we are the circumcision, who worship God in the Spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh; though I myself might have confidence even in the flesh. If any other man thinks that he has confidence in the flesh, I yet more:
slang_text: Finally, my brothers, be stoked in the Lord. Writin' the same stuff to you again ain't a drag for me, and it's safe for you to hear it twice. Watch out for those dogs, watch out for the evil workers, watch out for the fake circumcision crowd. 'Cause we're the real circumcision, worshippin' God in the Spirit, stoked in Christ Jesus, puttin' zero confidence in the flesh — even though I myself could totally lean on the flesh if I wanted. If any other dude thinks he's got somethin' to brag about in the flesh, I got even more:

web_text: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; concerning the law, a Pharisee; concerning zeal, persecuting the assembly; concerning the righteousness which is in the law, found blameless. However, I consider those things that were gain to me as a loss for Christ. Yes most certainly, and I count all things to be a loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus, my Lord, for whom I suffered the loss of all things, and count them nothing but refuse, that I may gain Christ
slang_text: circumcised on day eight, straight outta Israel's bloodline, tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew through and through; law-wise, a Pharisee; zeal-wise, I persecuted the church; righteousness-wise, by the law's own standard, straight-up blameless. But whatever I used to count as profit, I now count as a total loss, for Christ's sake. Yeah, no doubt about it, I count everything as a loss compared to how epic it is knowin' Christ Jesus my Lord — I lost everything for Him, and I count it all as trash, garbage, so I could gain Christ

web_text: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own, that which is of the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith; that I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed to his death; if by any means I may attain to the resurrection from the dead. Not that I have already obtained, or am already made perfect; but I press on, that I may take hold of that for which also I was taken hold of by Christ Jesus.
slang_text: and end up found in Him, not carryin' some righteousness I earned myself outta the law, but the righteousness that comes through faith in Christ, straight from God, by faith; so I could know Him, and the power of His resurrection, and the fellowship of sufferin' right alongside Him, gettin' shaped into His own death; hopin' somehow I'll reach the resurrection from the dead. Not that I've already grabbed hold of it, or already got made perfect — but I'm still pushin' forward, tryin' to grab onto the very thing Christ Jesus already grabbed me for.

web_text: Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do. Forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before, I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus. Let us therefore, as many as are perfect, think this way. If in anything you think otherwise, God will also reveal that to you. Nevertheless, to the extent that we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule. Let us be of the same mind.
slang_text: Brothers, I don't figure I've grabbed hold of it yet, but here's the one thing I'm doin': forgettin' everything behind me, stretchin' out toward what's ahead, I push forward toward the goal, chasin' the prize of God's high callin' in Christ Jesus. So let's all think this way, everybody who's matured in the faith. And if you're thinkin' different on somethin', God's gonna show you that too. Meanwhile, wherever we've already gotten to, let's keep walkin' by that same standard. Let's stay on the same page.

web_text: Brothers, be imitators together of me, and note those who walk this way, even as you have us for an example. For many walk, of whom I told you often, and now tell you even weeping, as the enemies of the cross of Christ, whose end is destruction, whose god is the belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who think about earthly things. For our citizenship is in heaven, from where we also wait for a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ;
slang_text: Brothers, copy me, all of you together, and keep an eye out for the ones walkin' this same way, matchin' the example we set for you. 'Cause a bunch of folks walk around — I've told you about 'em plenty, and now I'm tellin' you again, cryin' as I say it — actin' like enemies of Christ's cross, headed straight for destruction, worshippin' their own gut as their god, braggin' about stuff they oughta be ashamed of, mindin' nothin' but earthly junk. But our citizenship's up in heaven, and that's where we're waitin' for a Savior to come from, the Lord Jesus Christ;

web_text: who will change the body of our humiliation to be conformed to the body of his glory, according to the working by which he is able even to subject all things to himself.
slang_text: who's gonna transform this beat-up, humble body of ours to match His own glorious body, usin' that same power He's got to put absolutely everything under His control.

web_text: Therefore, my brothers, beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand firm in the Lord, my beloved. I exhort Euodia, and I exhort Syntyche, to think the same way in the Lord. Yes, I beg you also, true partner, help these women, for they labored with me in the Good News, with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the book of life. Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, “Rejoice!”
slang_text: So, my brothers, my beloved ones I'm achin' to see, my joy and my crown — stand firm in the Lord, my beloved. I'm pushin' Euodia, and I'm pushin' Syntyche, to get on the same page in the Lord. And yeah, I'm beggin' you too, my true partner, help these ladies out, 'cause they worked hard right alongside me for the Good News, along with Clement and the rest of my crew, whose names are already in the book of life. Be stoked in the Lord, always! I'll say it again: "Be stoked!"

web_text: Let your gentleness be known to all men. The Lord is at hand. In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus. Finally, brothers, whatever things are true, whatever things are honorable, whatever things are just, whatever things are pure, whatever things are lovely, whatever things are of good report; if there is any virtue, and if there is any praise, think about these things.
slang_text: Let your gentleness be somethin' everybody notices. The Lord's right around the corner. Don't stress out about nothin', but in everything, through prayer and requests, throw in some thanksgiving, and let God know what you're askin' for. And the peace of God, way past anything you could ever figure out, is gonna guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. Finally, brothers, whatever's true, whatever's honorable, whatever's fair, whatever's pure, whatever's lovely, whatever's got a good rep — if there's anything righteous, anything worth praisin', think on that stuff.

web_text: The things which you learned, received, heard, and saw in me: do these things, and the God of peace will be with you. But I rejoice in the Lord greatly, that now at length you have revived your thought for me; in which you did indeed take thought, but you lacked opportunity. Not that I speak because of lack, for I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content in it. I know how to be humbled, and I know also how to abound. In everything and in all things I have learned the secret both to be filled and to be hungry, both to abound and to be in need.
slang_text: Whatever you learned, received, heard, and saw in me — go do it, and the God of peace is gonna be with you. Man, I'm super stoked in the Lord that you finally got a chance to show you're still thinkin' of me — you always cared, you just didn't have the openin' before. Not that I'm sayin' this 'cause I'm hurtin' for somethin', 'cause I've learned to be content no matter what state I'm in. I know how to get by with nothin', and I know how to roll in plenty too. In every single situation, I've cracked the secret — bein' full or bein' hungry, havin' plenty or comin' up short.

web_text: I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me. However you did well that you shared in my affliction. You yourselves also know, you Philippians, that in the beginning of the Good News, when I departed from Macedonia, no assembly shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving but you only. For even in Thessalonica you sent once and again to my need.
slang_text: I can handle anything through Christ, who's the one pumpin' me up with strength. Still, you did good pitchin' in with my hard times. You Philippians already know, back when the Good News first started, when I split from Macedonia, not one single church shared with me on the give-and-take deal except you guys, only you. Even back in Thessalonica, you sent stuff my way, more than once, coverin' what I needed.

web_text: Not that I seek for the gift, but I seek for the fruit that increases to your account. But I have all things, and abound. I am filled, having received from Epaphroditus the things that came from you, a sweet-smelling fragrance, an acceptable and well-pleasing sacrifice to God. My God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus. Now to our God and Father be the glory forever and ever! Amen.
slang_text: Not that I'm chasin' the gift itself — I'm chasin' the fruit that's stackin' up in your favor. I've got everything I need, more than enough, honestly. I'm totally full up, gettin' what you sent through Epaphroditus, a sweet-smellin' offerin', a sacrifice God's totally pleased with and happy to accept. And my God's gonna cover every single need you've got, out of His riches in glory, in Christ Jesus. Now to our God and Father be the glory, forever and ever! Amen.

web_text: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me greet you. All the saints greet you, especially those who are of Caesar’s household. The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
slang_text: Give my greetin's to every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers here with me send their greetin's too. All the saints send their love, especially the ones from Caesar's own household. The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you. Amen.
